info.title: PMXT Hosted Router API
info.description: Hosted-only endpoints for cross-venue search, matching, arbitrage, and SQL.

tags.name: Trading (Hosted)
tags.description: Hosted trading endpoints. Build / submit / cancel orders via `trade.pmxt.dev/v0/*`. Hosted-mode is the default when `pmxt_api_key` is set.

tags.name: Orders & Positions (Hosted)
tags.description: Hosted account reads. Open orders, fills, balances, and positions via `trade.pmxt.dev/v0/*`. Requires `pmxt_api_key` + `wallet_address`.

tags.name: MatchedMarkets
tags.description: Cross-venue matched market and event clusters.

tags.name: SQL
tags.description: Direct read-only SQL access to the catalog (Enterprise).

post.description:
Build an order *without* placing it. Returns the order's typed-data payload (for the wallet to sign) plus a `built_order_id` to pass to `submitOrderHosted` once it's signed.

Use this when you want a human to approve each trade before it submits — for example, surfacing the order details in a wallet popup. For everything else, use `createOrderHosted`, which builds + signs + submits in one call.

200.description: Built order with typed data to sign.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

403.description: Insufficient escrow balance to back the requested order size.

404.description: Outcome not found in the catalog.

422.description: Invalid order parameters (e.g. price out of range, denom mismatch).

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# All hosted venues are funded once on Polygon — see /guides/escrow-lifecycle.
# Hosted writes need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address + private_key (signs EIP-712 locally).
# Pass an outcome straight from client.fetch_markets() — no UUID lookup needed.
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",    # EVM address — its Polygon USDC funds the escrow
    private_key="0x...",    # any EVM key controlling that address
)
market = client.fetch_markets({"query": "trump 2028"})[0]
yes = next(o for o in market.outcomes if o.label.lower() == "yes")
order = client.build_order(
    outcome=yes,
    side="buy",
    type="limit",
    amount=10,
    price=0.55,
)
print(order.id, order.status)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
Opinion

x-codeSamples.label: Opinion

x-mint.content:
<Info>
  **Available on:** Polymarket, Opinion. Other venues raise `NotSupported` in hosted mode — for those, run a [local PMXT service](/guides/self-hosted).
</Info>

<Note>
  **Before your first call:** deposit USDC on Polygon at [pmxt.dev/dashboard/wallet](https://pmxt.dev/dashboard/wallet) (one-time setup). For the programmatic flow, see [escrow lifecycle](/guides/escrow-lifecycle).
</Note>

<Tip>
  Use any EVM private key. Your USDC sits in a non-custodial PreFundedEscrow on Polygon — the single funding location for every hosted venue (including Opinion, which PMXT settles cross-chain for you). PMXT cannot move funds without your EIP-712 signature.
</Tip>

post.description:
Submit a signed order returned by `buildOrderHosted` and get back the resulting order — id, fill status, average price, and the on-chain tx hash once it settles.

`built_order_id` must come from a recent `buildOrderHosted` call and be submitted before its expiry. For one-shot order placement, use `createOrderHosted` instead.

200.description: Order accepted by the hosted backend.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

410.description: `built_order_id` expired before submission. Re-run `buildOrderHosted` and submit again.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# All hosted venues are funded once on Polygon — see /guides/escrow-lifecycle.
# Hosted writes need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address + private_key (signs EIP-712 locally).
# Pass an outcome straight from client.fetch_markets() — no UUID lookup needed.
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",    # EVM address — its Polygon USDC funds the escrow
    private_key="0x...",    # any EVM key controlling that address
)
market = client.fetch_markets({"query": "trump 2028"})[0]
built = client.build_order(
    outcome=market.yes,
    side="buy",
    type="limit",
    amount=10,
    price=0.55,
)
order = client.submit_order(built)
print(order.id, order.status)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
Opinion

post.description:
Place a buy or sell order on Polymarket or Opinion. Returns the resulting order with id, fill status, average price, fees, and the on-chain tx hash once it settles.

Most callers pass an outcome straight from `client.fetch_markets()`:

```python
market = client.fetch_markets({"query": "trump 2028"})[0]
order = client.create_order(outcome=market.yes, side="buy", amount=10, order_type="limit", price=0.55)
```

Need to show the order to a user before they sign it (custom approval UX)? Use `buildOrderHosted` + `submitOrderHosted` instead — together they do the same thing.

200.description: Order accepted by the hosted backend.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# All hosted venues are funded once on Polygon — see /guides/escrow-lifecycle.
# Hosted writes need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address + private_key (signs EIP-712 locally).
# Pass an outcome straight from client.fetch_markets() — no UUID lookup needed.
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",    # EVM address — its Polygon USDC funds the escrow
    private_key="0x...",    # any EVM key controlling that address
)
market = client.fetch_markets({"query": "trump 2028"})[0]
yes = next(o for o in market.outcomes if o.label.lower() == "yes")
order = client.create_order(
    outcome=yes,
    side="buy",
    type="limit",
    amount=10,
    price=0.55,
)
print(order.id, order.status)

post.description:
Start a cancel on an existing open order. Returns the cancel payload for the wallet to sign — you then submit the signature to `POST /v0/orders/cancel`.

The SDK's `cancelOrder()` chains both steps. Call this endpoint directly only if you need to show the cancel to a user before signing.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# All hosted venues are funded once on Polygon — see /guides/escrow-lifecycle.
# Hosted writes need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address + private_key (signs EIP-712 locally).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",    # EVM address — its Polygon USDC funds the escrow
    private_key="0x...",    # any EVM key controlling that address
)
cancelled = client.cancel_order("order_abc123")
print(cancelled.id, cancelled.status)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
Opinion

    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",    # EVM address — its Polygon USDC funds the escrow
    private_key="0x...",    # any EVM key controlling that address

cancelled = client.cancel_order("order_abc123")
print(cancelled.id, cancelled.status)

get.description:
Check the USDC available for trading. Returns the wallet's balance inside the PMXT escrow on Polygon — *not* the wallet's on-chain USDC balance.

Top up by depositing at [pmxt.dev/dashboard/wallet](https://pmxt.dev/dashboard/wallet).

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

503.description: Catalog or escrow indexer temporarily unavailable.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
balance = client.fetch_balance()
print(balance.amount, balance.currency)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
Opinion
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Opinion(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
balance = client.fetch_balance()
print(balance.amount, balance.currency)

get.description:
List the open positions held by `address` across Polymarket and Opinion. Each position carries the market, outcome, share count, and average entry price.

Mark-to-market fields (`current_price`, `current_value`, `unrealized_pnl`) are reserved for a future release and currently return `null`.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
for position in client.fetch_positions():
    print(position.market_id, position.shares, position.current_value)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
Opinion
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).

get.description:
List the wallet's resting orders that haven't filled or been cancelled yet. Each entry has the market, outcome, side, size, price, and time-in-force.

Filter by `venue` to scope to Polymarket or Opinion only.

parameters.name: venue
parameters.description: Restrict to a single hosted venue.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
for order in client.fetch_open_orders():
    print(order.id, order.side, order.price, order.remaining)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
Opinion
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).

    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
for order in client.fetch_open_orders():
    print(order.id, order.side, order.price, order.remaining)

get.description:
List the wallet's historical fills on Polymarket and Opinion. Each trade carries the executed price (net of venue fees), size, and on-chain settlement tx hash.

Closed orders are modelled as trades in hosted mode — use this endpoint instead of `fetchClosedOrders` / `fetchAllOrders` (which raise `NotSupported`).

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
for trade in client.fetch_my_trades():
    print(trade.id, trade.side, trade.amount, trade.price, trade.tx_hash)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
Opinion
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).

    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)

    print(trade.id, trade.side, trade.amount, trade.price, trade.tx_hash)

get.description:
Look up a single order by id. Returns the order's current status, filled / remaining size, average fill price, and — once settled — the on-chain tx hash.

`order_id` is what `createOrderHosted` (or `fetchOpenOrdersHosted`) returns.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
order = client.fetch_order("order_abc123")
print(order.id, order.status, order.filled, order.remaining)

x-codeSamples.label: Polymarket
Opinion
x-codeSamples.source:
# Hosted reads need: pmxt_api_key + wallet_address (private_key is only required for writes).

print(order.id, order.status, order.filled, order.remaining)

bearerAuth.description: Required when calling the hosted API directly (curl, requests, fetch). SDK users pass credentials via constructor params instead.

outcomeId.description: Catalog outcome UUID -- canonical across venues; the id you pass to `buildOrderHosted` / `createOrderHosted`.

marketId.description: Catalog UUID of the parent market this outcome belongs to.

label.description: Human-readable outcome name (e.g. `Yes`, `No`, or a candidate name in multi-outcome markets).

price.description: Last-traded probability in the [0, 1] range. `null` when the venue has not reported a recent trade.

priceChange24h.description: Absolute change in `price` over the trailing 24 hours, in probability units (e.g. `0.03` = +3 cents). `null` when 24h history is unavailable.

bestBid.description: Best bid on the venue's order book, in probability units [0, 1]. `null` when no bid is currently posted.

bestAsk.description: Best ask on the venue's order book, in probability units [0, 1]. `null` when no ask is currently posted.

metadata.description: Venue-specific passthrough fields (e.g. Polymarket `tokenId`, `negRisk`, `tickSize`) preserved so callers can drop down to venue-native APIs without a second catalog lookup.

marketId.description: Catalog market UUID -- canonical across venues.

eventId.description: Catalog UUID of the parent `UnifiedEvent`, when the market is part of a larger event cluster. `null` for standalone markets.

title.description: Human-readable market question (e.g. `Will BTC close above $100k by Dec 31?`).

slug.description: URL-safe identifier used by the source venue (e.g. Polymarket market slug).

description.description: Long-form market description / resolution rules text as provided by the venue. `null` when the venue does not expose one.

url.description: Canonical link to the market on the source venue's website.

image.description: URL of the venue-provided market image / thumbnail.

category.description: Venue-assigned category (e.g. `Politics`, `Crypto`, `Sports`). Not normalized across venues.

tags.description: Free-form tags assigned by the source venue.

volume.description: Lifetime traded volume on the source venue, in USDC dollars. `null` when the venue does not report it.

volume24h.description: Traded volume over the trailing 24 hours on the source venue, in USDC dollars.

liquidity.description: Resting order-book liquidity reported by the venue, in USDC dollars.

openInterest.description: Outstanding share-equivalent open interest, in shares. `null` when the venue does not expose it.

resolutionDate.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the market is expected to resolve. `null` if the venue has not committed to a resolution date.

tickSize.description: Minimum price increment on the venue's order book, in probability units (e.g. `0.01` = 1 cent). `null` when not exposed.

status.description: Lifecycle status as reported by the venue (e.g. `active`, `closed`, `resolved`). Values are not normalized across venues.

contractAddress.description: On-chain contract address backing the market (e.g. Polymarket CTF exchange). `null` for off-chain venues.

outcomes.description: Outcomes the user can take a position on. Binary markets contain two entries (`Yes` / `No`); multi-outcome markets contain one entry per candidate.

id.description: Catalog event UUID -- groups related markets (e.g. all races within a single election).

title.description: Human-readable event name (e.g. `2028 US Presidential Election`).

description.description: Long-form event description from the source venue. `null` when unavailable.

slug.description: URL-safe identifier used by the source venue.

markets.description: Markets that belong to this event.

volume24h.description: Aggregate 24h traded volume across all markets in the event, in USDC dollars. `null` when unavailable.

volume.description: Aggregate lifetime traded volume across all markets in the event, in USDC dollars. `null` when unavailable.

url.description: Canonical link to the event on the source venue's website.

image.description: URL of the venue-provided event image / thumbnail.

category.description: Venue-assigned category (e.g. `Politics`). Not normalized across venues.

tags.description: Free-form tags assigned by the source venue.

count.description: Number of items returned in this response page.

limit.description: Maximum number of items the caller requested per page.

offset.description: Zero-based offset of the first item in this page relative to the full result set.

error.description: Human-readable error message. Use `HostedErrorResponse` instead for hosted endpoints, which carries a stable machine-readable `code`.

plan.description: PMXT plan name in effect for the API key (e.g. `free`, `pro`, `enterprise`).

limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in the current `window`.

used.description: Number of requests already consumed in the current `window`.

HostedErrorResponse.description: Error envelope returned by `trade.pmxt.dev/v0/*` endpoints. Mirrors the `ErrorDetail` shape used inside `BaseResponse.error` from the sidecar spec.

error.description: Structured error envelope. Always present on non-2xx responses.

message.description: Human-readable explanation safe to surface to end users.

code.description: Stable hosted-mode error code. Use this (not `message`) for branching in client code.

retryable.description: `true` when the same request may succeed if retried (e.g. transient catalog outage); `false` when the caller must change inputs first.

exchange.description: Source venue that produced the error (e.g. `polymarket`, `opinion`), when the failure originated upstream of PMXT. `null` for PMXT-side errors.

detail.description: Free-form structured diagnostics (e.g. required minimum size, escrow balance available). Shape varies per `code`.

BuildOrderHostedRequest.description: Hosted build-order request. Identify the target outcome by passing `venue` + `venue_outcome_id` from `client.fetch_markets()` (the SDK does this automatically when you pass `outcome=` to `create_order` or `build_order`).

venue.description: Venue the outcome trades on. Inferred automatically from your client class in the SDKs.

venue_outcome_id.description: The outcome's identifier (e.g. Polymarket `tokenId`, Opinion outcome hash). Returned by `client.fetch_markets()`.

side.description: Direction of the order. `buy` opens or adds to a long position on the outcome; `sell` closes or reduces it.

order_type.description: `market` fills immediately at the best available price (subject to `slippage_pct`); `limit` rests on the venue's order book at `price` until matched or cancelled.

amount.description: Order size. For `market` buys, in USDC dollars (the budget you want to spend). For `market` sells and all `limit` orders, in outcome shares.

denom.description: Unit `amount` is denominated in. `shares` = outcome shares; `usdc` = USDC dollars. Market buys require `usdc`; market sells and limit orders require `shares` (the server validates this combination).

price.description: Required for `limit` orders. Probability in [0, 1] -- e.g. `0.55` means buying / selling shares at 55 cents each. Ignored for `market` orders.

slippage_pct.description: Maximum acceptable slippage as a percent. Use aggressive defaults (`30` for buys, `99.9` for sells) until the upstream economic validator tightens -- lower values frequently trip precision checks. Ignored for market orders, which pin worst-price to the domain extreme; the server defaults to `20` when omitted.

user_address.description: EVM wallet address that will sign the resulting typed data. Must match the wallet whose USDC funded the PMXT PreFundedEscrow on Polygon.

BuildOrderHostedResponse.description: Hosted build-order response. The caller must sign `typed_data` locally (and `pull_typed_data` if present) and POST the signatures back via `submitOrderHosted` before the order expires.

built_order_id.description: Opaque server-side key used by `submitOrderHosted` to look up the build context.

side.description: Echo of the order side from the request.

typed_data.description: EIP-712 typed-data payload to sign locally with the wallet key matching `user_address`. Return the signature in `SubmitOrderHostedRequest.signature`.

pull_typed_data.description: Optional secondary EIP-712 payload for venues that require a separate pull-authorization (notably Polymarket neg-risk markets and sell orders). Sign with the same wallet and return in `SubmitOrderHostedRequest.pull_signature`. `null` when not required.

quote.description: Pre-trade quote: expected average fill price, slippage, and fees.

best_price.description: Top-of-book price on the side you are trading against (best ask for buys, best bid for sells), in probability units [0, 1].

expected_avg_price.description: Volume-weighted average fill price across the order-book levels that would be consumed, in probability units.

expected_slippage_pct.description: Expected slippage from `best_price` to `expected_avg_price`, expressed as a percent.

estimated_cost_or_proceeds.description: Estimated USDC dollars to be spent (for buys) or received (for sells), net of fees.

fillable.description: `true` when the venue currently has enough resting liquidity to fill the requested size at-or-better than the implied worst price.

liquidity.description: Total resting liquidity on the relevant book side, in USDC dollars.

fee_amount.description: Estimated PMXT + venue fee for the order, in USDC dollars.

tick_size.description: Minimum price increment on the venue's order book, as a decimal string (e.g. `"0.01"`).

resolved.description: Venue-side fields resolved from the supplied outcome — token ids, contract addresses, etc. Useful when you want to cross-reference the order against the venue's own API. `null` if resolution failed.

venue.description: Venue the order will execute on.

token_id.description: Venue-native outcome identifier (Polymarket ERC-1155 `tokenId`, or Opinion outcome hash).

neg_risk.description: `true` when the market uses Polymarket's neg-risk contract (which requires the secondary `pull_typed_data` signature).

tick_size.description: Minimum price increment on the venue's order book, in probability units (e.g. `0.01`).

opinion_market_id.description: Opinion-native integer market id. `null` for Polymarket orders.

SubmitOrderHostedRequest.description: Hosted submit-order request. `signature` is the local EIP-712 signature over `BuildOrderHostedResponse.typed_data`.

built_order_id.description: Opaque key returned by `buildOrderHosted`. Identifies the server-side build context to submit against.

signature.description: Hex-encoded EIP-712 signature over `BuildOrderHostedResponse.typed_data`, produced locally with the wallet key matching `user_address`.

pull_signature.description: Hex-encoded EIP-712 signature over `BuildOrderHostedResponse.pull_typed_data`. Required when the build response returned a non-null `pull_typed_data` (Polymarket neg-risk markets and sell orders); `null` otherwise.

wait.description: When `true`, the server blocks until on-chain settlement before responding (returns the populated `tx_hash`). When `false`, returns immediately with the in-flight order.

order_id.description: Unified order id, as returned by `fetchOpenOrdersHosted`.

user_address.description: EVM wallet address that owns the resting order and will sign the cancel typed data.

cancel_id.description: Opaque server-side key for this cancel build. Pass it to `POST /v0/orders/cancel` with the EIP-712 signature(s) before `deadline`.

typed_data.description: EIP-712 typed-data payload to sign locally with the wallet key matching `user_address`. Return the signature when submitting the cancel.

pull_typed_data.description: Optional secondary EIP-712 payload for venues that require a pull-authorization cancel (Polymarket neg-risk markets). Sign with the same wallet and return as `pull_signature`. `null` when not required.

deadline.description: Unix epoch (s) after which the cancel build expires. Submit the signed cancel before this time or call `cancelOrderHosted` again.

OrderV0.description: Hosted-mode `Order` shape. Mirrors `pmxt.Order` so the SDK can return it directly. `tx_hash`, `chain`, and `block_number` populate once execution settles on-chain.

id.description: Unified order id. Stable across the order's lifetime; reuse it in `fetchOrderHosted` and `cancelOrderHosted`.

side.description: Order direction. `null` on cancel responses, which only carry id and status.

type.description: Order type echoed from the build. `null` on cancel responses.

amount.description: Order size in outcome shares. For market submits, this is the shares actually obtained (`tokens_bought` / `tokens_sold`); for resting limit orders it is the total shares originally requested. `null` on cancel responses.

price.description: Limit price in probability units [0, 1] for limit orders. `null` for market orders and cancel responses.

filled.description: Shares filled so far. For market submits this equals the shares obtained on-chain; for resting limit orders it is the running fill total.

remaining.description: Shares still outstanding (`amount - filled`, floored at 0). Reaches 0 when the order is fully filled or cancelled.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Open-order values include `resting` and `partial`; submit responses pass through the upstream status string (`failed` when an error was raised); cancel responses return the venue's cancel-acknowledgement status.

fee.description: Total fee charged for this order, in USDC dollars. `null` until the venue reports fees (typically after settlement).

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the order was created on the venue side. `null` on cancel responses.

tx_hash.description: On-chain settlement transaction hash on Polygon. `null` until the order settles on-chain (resting limit orders stay `null` until matched).

chain.description: Chain the order settled on. Always `polygon` for hosted orders today (Opinion settles cross-chain via the same Polygon escrow). `null` on cancel responses.

block_number.description: Polygon block height at which the order settled. `null` until settlement.

id.description: Venue-issued trade id. `null` when the venue does not expose a stable id for this fill.

side.description: Direction of the fill from the wallet's perspective.

amount.description: Shares filled by this trade (`fill.shares` from the operator's fill record).

price.description: Net average fill price, in probability units [0, 1] -- already includes per-fill venue fees (`fill.avg_price_net`).

fee.description: Sum of all fee components on the fill (venue + PMXT), in USDC dollars. `null` when the operator did not record fee components.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the trade as recorded by the operator.

tx_hash.description: Polygon settlement transaction hash. Prefers the settlement-tx hash when present; falls back to the first underlying fill transaction.

venue.description: Source venue that produced the fill.

PositionV0.description: Hosted-mode `Position` shape. `current_price`, `current_value`, `entry_price`, `realized_pnl`, and `outcome_label` may be null when the server doesn't yet have the data.

venue.description: Venue the position is held on. Defaults to `polymarket` for tokens whose source venue could not be inferred.

shares.description: Outcome shares held -- the ERC-1155 (or Opinion-native) token balance held by the PMXT PreFundedEscrow on behalf of the wallet.

current_price.description: Current mark price in probability units [0, 1]. Always `null` in this release -- server-side orderbook mark-to-market is not yet batched (even with `with_mtm=true`).

current_value.description: Current mark-to-market value in USDC dollars (`shares * current_price`). Always `null` in this release for the same reason as `current_price`.

outcome_label.description: Human-readable outcome name (e.g. `Yes`), enriched from the user's recorded buy fills. `null` when the user has no fill history for this token in the operator DB.

entry_price.description: Cost-basis approximation: sum(buy USDC) / sum(buy shares) across all of the user's buy fills for this token, in USDC-per-share. Ignores sells -- lot-level matching is not yet implemented. `null` when there are no recorded buy fills.

realized_pnl.description: Always `null` in this release. Derivation requires lot-level matching of sell fills, which is not yet implemented.

BalanceV0.description: Hosted-mode `Balance` shape. Returns USDC held inside the PMXT `PreFundedEscrow` contract.

currency.description: Currency code. Always `USDC` -- the only collateral accepted by the hosted escrow.

amount.description: Spendable USDC dollars held inside the PMXT PreFundedEscrow on Polygon (NOT the venue-native CLOB-proxy balance). Backs trading across every hosted venue, including Opinion.

venue.description: Set when the hosted backend returns a per-venue breakdown; null when balance is venue-agnostic.
